mnemonics: Universal vs. Existential Quantifiers are like 'All' and 'Some' in everyday language.
For-All (Quantifier) is like a Rule: All students must attend classes.
keyPoints: The universal quantifier (∀) applies to every element in a set.
The existential quantifier (∃) applies to at least one element in a set.
The use of quantifiers helps to express mathematical relationships between sets of elements.
